attrs.alt: Azure AD B2C vs Microsoft Entra External ID comparison

content.text: Microsoft currently offers two customer identity and access management (CIAM) platforms: the original Azure Active Directory B2C (B2C), launched in 2015, and the newer Microsoft Entra External ID, which became generally available in 2024. Understanding when to use each — and how to migrate between them — is essential for identity architects working with consumer-facing applications.

Azure AD B2C is not deprecated. Microsoft has committed to supporting it through at least May 2031. However, new CIAM features are delivered exclusively in Microsoft Entra External ID. B2C is in a maintenance-only phase for bug fixes and security updates.
External tenant inside Entra ID hierarchy
No — not available in External ID
Custom CSS + logo/background; no full page replacement yet
When B2C Still Has an Advantage
Despite being the older platform, B2C retains genuine advantages in specific scenarios that External ID does not yet support:

Identity Experience Framework (IEF): Custom XML policies enabling arbitrarily complex journey logic — step-up auth, conditional branching between many IdPs, legacy system password migration, and multi-step claim enrichment chains.
Full HTML page replacement: B2C allows replacing the entire sign-in page with your own hosted HTML (with B2C's JS injected). External ID currently supports only CSS customization and asset uploads.
SAML SP-initiated flows: B2C can issue SAML assertions to SAML-only relying parties. External ID's SAML support is less mature.
Proven scale: B2C has been in production at scale since 2015; organizations with strict reliability track-record requirements may prefer it for high-stakes consumer apps.
When to Choose External ID for New Projects
You don't need IEF custom policies — user flows cover your sign-up/sign-in requirements.
You want unified administration alongside your workforce Entra ID tenant (same portal, same Conditional Access policies, same audit logs).
You need passkeys/FIDO2 or native authentication APIs for iOS/Android apps (External ID only).
You want to leverage the Microsoft Entra security roadmap — MFA resilience, Continuous Access Evaluation, and advanced identity protection are being invested in External ID first.
You are building a greenfield application with no legacy B2C dependency.
Migration Path: B2C to External ID

Microsoft does not provide an automated tenant-to-tenant migration tool. Migration requires re-provisioning the identity infrastructure in an External ID tenant and migrating users. The typical approach is a phased migration using the 'just-in-time' user migration pattern.
Phase 1: Provision External ID Tenant and User Flows

# In the Entra admin center, create an External ID tenant:
# Azure Portal -> Microsoft Entra ID -> External Identities -> Create a tenant

# Once created, switch context and configure user flows
Connect-MgGraph -TenantId 'contoso-ext.onmicrosoft.com' -Scopes 'Policy.ReadWrite.TrustFramework'

# Recreate sign-up/sign-in user flow in External ID
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST `
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/b2cUserFlows' `
    -Body (@{
        '@odata.type'       = '#microsoft.graph.b2cIdentityUserFlow'
        id                  = 'B2C_1_SignUpSignIn'
        userFlowType        = 'signUpOrSignIn'
        userFlowTypeVersion = 2
    } | ConvertTo-Json) `
    -ContentType 'application/json'
Write-Host 'External ID user flow created.'

Rather than bulk-migrating password hashes (which is often impossible — B2C hashes are not exportable), use the just-in-time pattern: the first time a user signs in after the migration, a custom policy or API connector validates their credential against the legacy B2C system and creates the user in External ID.

# Export user metadata (not passwords) from B2C for bulk pre-creation in External ID
# Connect to source B2C tenant
Connect-MgGraph -TenantId 'contoso-b2c.onmicrosoft.com' -Scopes 'User.ReadBasic.All'

$b2cUsers = Get-MgUser -All `
    -Property 'id,displayName,mail,identities,createdDateTime'

$export = $b2cUsers | ForEach-Object {
    @{
        displayName  = $_.DisplayName
        mail         = $_.Mail
        identities   = $_.Identities
        b2cObjectId  = $_.Id
        migrated     = $false
    }
}

$export | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5 | Out-File './migration/b2c-users.json' -Encoding UTF8
Write-Host "Exported $($b2cUsers.Count) users for migration planning."
Phase 3: Update Application Redirect URIs and Authority

# B2C authority (old):
# https://contoso-b2c.b2clogin.com/contoso-b2c.onmicrosoft.com/B2C_1_SignUpSignIn/v2.0

# External ID authority (new):
# https://contoso-ext.ciamlogin.com/contoso-ext.onmicrosoft.com/v2.0

# Key differences in the OIDC configuration:
# 1. Authority URL changes from b2clogin.com to ciamlogin.com
# 2. The policy parameter (p=) is no longer required in External ID
# 3. Scope format: openid offline_access api://<client-id>/read (same pattern)

# Update app configuration (example for Python MSAL):
# OLD:
# AUTHORITY = 'https://contoso.b2clogin.com/tfp/contoso.onmicrosoft.com/B2C_1_SignUpSignIn'

# NEW:
# AUTHORITY = 'https://contoso-ext.ciamlogin.com/contoso-ext.onmicrosoft.com'

For large production B2C tenants, a hard cutover is too risky. A typical coexistence approach runs both tenants in parallel — new user registrations go to External ID while existing B2C users continue to authenticate against B2C until they are migrated just-in-time.
First sign-in migrates to External ID; subsequent sign-ins via External ID

// B2C sign-in volume by result — last 30 days
SignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where AuthenticationProtocol == 'oAuth2' or AuthenticationProtocol == 'oidc'
| summarize Total = count(), Failures = countif(ResultType != 0)
    by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
| extend SuccessRate = round(100.0 * (Total - Failures) / Total, 1)
| order by TimeGenerated desc

// External ID uses the same SignInLogs schema — filter by tenant
SignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where ResourceTenantId == '<external-id-tenant-id>'
| summarize Total = count(), Failures = countif(ResultType != 0)
    by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
| extend SuccessRate = round(100.0 * (Total - Failures) / Total, 1)
| order by TimeGenerated desc

App registration in B2C and External ID use the same Microsoft Graph API (/applications endpoint), but the authority URLs and OIDC metadata document locations differ. Understanding these differences is critical when migrating or running side-by-side configurations.
B2C App Registration and OIDC Discovery

# Register an app in a B2C tenant
Connect-MgGraph -TenantId 'contoso.onmicrosoft.com' -Scopes 'Application.ReadWrite.All'

$b2cApp = New-MgApplication -BodyParameter @{
    DisplayName    = 'ContosoApp-B2C'
    SignInAudience = 'AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount'
    Web = @{
        RedirectUris = @('https://app.contoso.com/signin-oidc')
    }
}
Write-Host "B2C App ID: $($b2cApp.AppId)"

# B2C OIDC discovery document URL (per-policy):
# https://contoso.b2clogin.com/contoso.onmicrosoft.com/B2C_1_SignUpSignIn/v2.0/.well-known/openid-configuration

# B2C token endpoint:
# https://contoso.b2clogin.com/contoso.onmicrosoft.com/B2C_1_SignUpSignIn/oauth2/v2.0/token
External ID App Registration and OIDC Discovery

# Register an app in an External ID tenant
Connect-MgGraph -TenantId 'contoso-ext.onmicrosoft.com' -Scopes 'Application.ReadWrite.All'

$extApp = New-MgApplication -BodyParameter @{
    DisplayName    = 'ContosoApp-ExternalID'
    SignInAudience = 'AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount'
    Web = @{
        RedirectUris = @('https://app.contoso.com/signin-oidc')
    }
}
Write-Host "External ID App ID: $($extApp.AppId)"

# External ID OIDC discovery document URL (no policy parameter):
# https://contoso-ext.ciamlogin.com/contoso-ext.onmicrosoft.com/v2.0/.well-known/openid-configuration

# External ID token endpoint:
# https://contoso-ext.ciamlogin.com/contoso-ext.onmicrosoft.com/oauth2/v2.0/token

Microsoft Entra External ID introduces the native authentication API — a direct REST API that allows mobile and desktop apps to implement authentication without browser redirects. This eliminates the need to open a web view or system browser for sign-in, enabling fully native UI experiences.
The native authentication API is available only in External ID. Azure AD B2C does not support it. This is one of the strongest reasons to choose External ID for new mobile consumer apps.
Native Auth Sign-in Flow (Python MSAL Preview)

# pip install msal
import msal

# External ID native auth configuration
TENANT_ID   = 'contoso-ext.onmicrosoft.com'
CLIENT_ID   = '<your-external-id-app-client-id>'
AUTHORITY   = f'https://contoso-ext.ciamlogin.com/{TENANT_ID}'

# Create a public client application
app = msal.PublicClientApplication(
    client_id=CLIENT_ID,
    authority=AUTHORITY,
    # Enable native auth (preview)
    oidc_authority=AUTHORITY,
)

# Initiate username/password sign-in (native, no browser redirect)
username = 'customer@example.com'
password = '<user-entered-password>'

result = app.acquire_token_by_username_password(
    username=username,
    password=password,
    scopes=['openid', 'offline_access'],
)

if 'access_token' in result:
    print('Sign-in successful')
    print(f'Token: {result["access_token"][:50]}...')
else:
    print(f'Error: {result.get("error_description")}')
Conditional Access: B2C vs External ID
Both platforms support Conditional Access policies, but the integration depth differs significantly.
Full CA policy with conditions (location, device, risk)
Identity Protection risk signals (requires Entra P2)
Identity Protection user risk (requires Entra P2)
Yes — block/allow by IP range or country
Yes — require Intune-compliant or Entra-joined device
Native CA terms of use integration
Yes — step-up MFA for sensitive operations
Creating a CA Policy in External ID (PowerShell)
Creating a Consumer User in B2C
Creating a Consumer User in External ID
Both platforms use the same azuread Terraform provider for app registrations. The difference is the provider's tenant_id value and, for B2C, the separate azurerm_aadb2c_directory resource.
Azure AD B2C and Microsoft Entra External ID are both GA CIAM platforms; B2C is in maintenance mode while External ID is the strategic investment.
B2C retains key advantages: Identity Experience Framework (IEF) custom policies, full HTML page replacement, and a longer production track record.
External ID offers native auth APIs (iOS/Android), passkeys, unified Entra admin, and full Conditional Access integration — features B2C will not receive.
Choose External ID for new projects unless you specifically need IEF custom policies or full HTML page control.
Migration from B2C to External ID is manual and phased — typically using just-in-time password migration since B2C password hashes are not exportable.
Microsoft has committed to B2C support through at least May 2031, so existing B2C tenants have time to plan a migration.
